{axd_dimension_s}
depends on serialization format context.

[[axd_dimension]]
{axd_dimension}::
A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
{axl_annotation}.
{axd_dimension_s}
appear after the primary
{axl_motif}
in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`

=== Value Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).

[[axd_required]]
{axd_required}::
Member or argument must be present.
Absence triggers validation error or
{axc_fatal}.

[[axd_optional]]
{axd_optional}::
Member or argument may be omitted.
Absence is valid and handled gracefully.

[[axd_nullable]]
{axd_nullable}::
Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.

[[axd_none]]
{axd_none}::
Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.

[[axd_defaulted]]
{axd_defaulted}::
Has fallback value when absent.
Voicing should specify the default value in prose.

[[axd_repeated]]
{axd_repeated}::
May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.

[[axd_list]]
{axd_list}::
Single value containing delimited multiple values.
Distinct from
{axd_repeated}
which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).

=== Format-Specific Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
apply only to specific serialization formats.
Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.

[[axd_field_number]]
{axd_field_number}::
Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_wire_type]]
{axd_wire_type}::
Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_endianness]]
{axd_endianness}::
Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
Required for binary format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.

[[axd_xml_form]]
{axd_xml_form}::
Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
Required for XML format.
Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.

=== Lifecycle Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axo_procedure}
voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
{axo_procedure}
voicing.
Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.

[[axd_transient]]
{axd_transient}::
{axo_procedure}
executes and completes.
The default lifecycle for most procedures.
{axhoc_completion}
documents exit status or return value.

[[axd_longrunning]]
{axd_longrunning}::
{axo_procedure}
runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
{axhoc_completion}
documents startup success criteria, not termination.
Requires
{axhos_step} (startup)
and
{axhos_step} (shutdown)
sections.
May include
{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
sections.

[[axd_periodic]]
{axd_periodic}::
{axo_procedure}
executes on a schedule.
{axhoc_completion}
applies per-run.
Requires
{axhos_step} (schedule)
section.
May include
{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
sections.

=== Regime Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify regime structure annotations.

[[axd_conditional]]
{axd_conditional}::
Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
A group annotated with
{axd_conditional}
requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
{axhrgc_gate}
specifying the activation criteria.

[[axrd_singleton]]
{axrd_singleton}::
Regime has exactly one assignment source.
No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.

[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Dimension Applicability

Per-format guidance for
{axd_dimension}
usage:

[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
|===
|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML

|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
|===

Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.

== Axial Format Terms

{axf_format_s}
specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use a particular
{axf_format}
to encode
{axrg_variable}
values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.

[[axf_format]]
{axf_format}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
{axf_format_s}
determine how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are written to and read from files.

[[axf_bash]]
{axf_bash}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing bash script format for configuration.
Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.

[[axf_makefile]]
{axf_makefile}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing makefile format for configuration.
Files are includable via `include file`.
Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.

[[axf_json]]
{axf_json}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing JSON format for configuration.
Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
Parsed by standard JSON libraries.

[[axf_yaml]]
{axf_yaml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing YAML format for configuration.
Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.

== Axial Regime Terms

{axrg_regime_s}
are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.

[[axrg_regime]]
{axrg_regime}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a structured configuration system.
A
{axrg_regime}
binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
The specification defines what can be configured;
{axrg_assignment_s}
provide specific values.

[[axrg_variable]]
{axrg_variable}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named configuration element at schema level.
Each
{axrg_variable}
belongs to an
{axrg_regime}
and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
{axrg_variable_s}
are the "what can be configured" definitions;
{axrg_assignment_s}
bind values to them.

[[axrg_assignment]]
{axrg_assignment}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a specific value bound to an
{axrg_variable}.
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried in files of a particular
{axf_format}
(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
{axrg_regime},
each providing different configuration instances.

[[axrg_prefix]]
{axrg_prefix}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
{axrg_variable_s}
within an
{axrg_regime}.
Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
Once established, an
{axrg_prefix}
becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
Changing an
{axrg_prefix}
requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.

== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions

Provenance dimensions qualify
{axrg_assignment}
to declare how values arrive at
{axvr_kindle}
time.
These are valid dimensions on
{axvr_regime}
declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.

[[axrd_file_sourced]]
{axrd_file_sourced}::
Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.

[[axrd_env_inherited]]
{axrd_env_inherited}::
Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.

[[axrd_constructed]]
{axrd_constructed}::
Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.

== Axial Regime Annotation Terms

Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)

{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[rbrv_sigil]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrv_sigil}::
Vessel identifier for build configurations.
----

The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the definition text that follows.

[[axvr_regime]]
{axvr_regime}::
Voices a regime definition.
The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axf_format}
(specifying how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried).

[[axvr_variable]]
{axvr_variable}::
Voices a regime variable definition.
The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional},
type
{axl_motif}
(base type or type voicing).

[[axvr_group]]
{axvr_group}::
Voices a regime group definition.
The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
{axrg_variable_s}
within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_conditional}
(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).

[[axvr_kindle]]
{axvr_kindle}::
Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.

[[axvr_validate]]
{axvr_validate}::
Voices a regime validate operation definition.
The annotated term defines the strict verification phase for a
{axrg_regime}:
checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.

[[axvr_render]]
{axvr_render}::
Voices a regime render operation definition.
The annotated term defines the diagnostic display phase for a
{axrg_regime}:
producing human-readable presentation of current state.
Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.

[[axvr_list]]
{axvr_list}::
Voices a regime list operation definition.
The annotated term defines an enumeration operation for an
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}:
listing available instances.

[[axvr_survey]]
{axvr_survey}::
Voices a regime survey operation definition.
The annotated term defines a read-only cross-instance summary operation for an
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

[[axvr_audit]]
{axvr_audit}::
Voices a regime audit operation definition.
The annotated term defines a cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for an
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)

Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text to establish hierarchical context.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhrb_regime
{rbrv_regime}

//axhrgv_variable
{rbrv_sigil}
----

The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
Following text contains the
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that the marker reads ahead.

==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
|===

[[axhrb_regime]]
{axhrb_regime}::
Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the regime this subdoc describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvr_regime}
in the parent document.

[[axhrv_variable]]
{axhrv_variable}::
Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the variable being detailed.

[[axhrgb_group]]
{axhrgb_group}::
Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the group being detailed.
The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
{axvr_group}
in the parent document.

[[axhrgc_gate]]
{axhrgc_gate}::
Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
Lookahead: 2
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
— the enumerated variable (voicing
{axt_enumeration})
and the specific
{axt_enum_value}
that activates this gate.
The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).

[[axhrgv_variable]]
{axhrgv_variable}::
Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.

[[axhro_kindle]]
{axhro_kindle}::
Marks a regime kindle operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level kindle operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_kindle}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_validate]]
{axhro_validate}::
Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_validate}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_render]]
{axhro_render}::
Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_render}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_list]]
{axhro_list}::
Marks a regime list operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level list operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_list}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

[[axhro_survey]]
{axhro_survey}::
Marks a regime survey operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level survey operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_survey}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

[[axhro_audit]]
{axhro_audit}::
Marks a regime audit operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level audit operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_audit}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

==== Nesting Rules

Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
* {axhrgb_group},
{axhrgc_gate},
and
{axhrgv_variable}
must appear within
{axhrb_regime}
scope
* {axhrgc_gate}
and
{axhrgv_variable}
must appear within
{axhrgb_group}
scope
* Multiple
{axhrgc_gate}
within one group have AND semantics
* {axhro_kindle},
{axhro_validate},
{axhro_render},
{axhro_list},
{axhro_survey},
and
{axhro_audit}
must appear within
{axhrb_regime}
scope but outside any
{axhrgb_group}
scope

==== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhrb_regime}
attribute reference must match a regime defined with
{axvr_regime}
in parent
* {axhrgb_group}
attribute reference must match a group defined with
{axvr_group}
under that regime
* {axhrgc_gate}
first attribute reference must voice
{axt_enumeration};
second must be an
{axt_enum_value}
of that specific enumeration type

== Axial Lexicon Structural Terms

[[axl_definition_site]]
{axl_definition_site}::
The location in a parent document where a linked term is defined: anchor, voicing annotation, and definition text.
The
{axl_definition_site}
establishes the term's identity, role, and architectural relationships.
Voicing annotations at the
{axl_definition_site}
may impose positional requirements on linked terms in the definition text that follows.

[[axl_detail_site]]
{axl_detail_site}::
The location where a linked term's details are elaborated through hierarchy markers and prose.
Typically a subdocument included from the parent, but may be inline within the parent document for compact definitions.
The
{axl_detail_site}
is opened by a hierarchy block marker (e.g.,
{axhob_operation},
{axhrb_regime})
and contains subordinate markers for parameters, steps, variables, or other structural elements.

== Axial Voicing Operation Terms

Two annotation families enable operation specification across
{axl_definition_site_s}
and
{axl_detail_site_s},
paralleling the regime annotation pattern.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvo_*)

{axvo_*} annotations appear at the
{axl_definition_site}
between anchor and definition, declaring the operation role of the term being defined.

[[axvo_procedure]]
{axvo_procedure}::
Voices an
{axo_procedure}
definition.
The annotated term defines an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
No entity affiliation required.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the procedure being defined.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
exactly one lifecycle
({axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}).
Optional
{axd_attended}.
Optional
{axd_internal}.
Optional
{axd_grouped}
(the second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be a term voiced
{axvo_group}).

[[axvo_method]]
{axvo_method}::
Voices an
{axo_method}
definition.
The annotated term defines an executable operation belonging to an entity's interface.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the method being defined.
The second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
{axo_entity}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
exactly one lifecycle
({axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}).
Optional
{axd_attended}.
Optional
{axd_internal}.
Optional
{axd_grouped}
(the third
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be a term voiced
{axvo_group}).

[[axvo_group]]
{axvo_group}::
Voices an
{axo_group}
definition.
The annotated term defines a named collection of related operations that share an affiliated entity.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the group being defined.
The second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
{axo_entity}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
optional
{axd_tabtarget},
{axd_slash_command},
or other exposure mechanism specifying the default interface for external members.

=== New Operation Dimension Terms

[[axd_attended]]
{axd_attended}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method requires human interaction during execution.
Absence means unattended (fully automated).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_internal]]
{axd_internal}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method is an implementation building block, not a consumer-facing interface.
Absence means external (published interface).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_grouped]]
{axd_grouped}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method belongs to a named operation group.
Imposes a positional requirement on the definition text: the next
{mcm_attribute_reference}
after any prior positional requirements must be an
{axo_group}.
For
{axvo_procedure}:
the second linked term must be the group.
For
{axvo_method}:
the third linked term must be the group (second is the entity).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_tabtarget]]
{axd_tabtarget}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as tabtargets.
Valid on
{axvo_group}.

[[axd_slash_command]]
{axd_slash_command}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as
{axi_cc_slash_command_s}.
Valid on
{axvo_group}.

[[axd_fact]]
{axd_fact}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that an operation output is delivered as a single atomic value in a well-known named file.
The file is written to the dispatch output directory; consumers read the value verbatim.
The constant name (carried by the marker's existing lookahead) is the contract between producer and consumer.
Valid on
{axhoot_typed_output}.
At the definition site, the corresponding term is voiced
{axpof_fact}.

[[axd_tally]]
{axd_tally}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that an operation output is a fact map — a dynamically-named set of fact files sharing a common infix.
The infix constant is the contract; surrounding filename segments are runtime-composed keys.
Consumers check file presence or glob by infix rather than reading a single well-known filename.
Valid on
{axhoot_typed_output}.
At the definition site, the corresponding term is voiced
{axpot_tally}.

[[axd_algebraic]]
{axd_algebraic}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating seed-derived positional encoding for an
{axt_insignia}.
Characters drawn from a fixed alphabet, fixed digit count, monotonic seed incremented under lock.
Digit count disambiguates type within the algebraic family.
Hierarchical embedding via prefix substring: a child identity's leading characters are the parent identity's value.

[[axd_temporal]]
{axd_temporal}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating time-derived encoding for an
{axt_insignia}.
Datestamp component plus autonumber (e.g., YYMMDD-NNNN), allocated by filesystem enumeration.
No hierarchical embedding.

=== Product-of-Operation Terms

[[axpof_fact]]
{axpof_fact}::
Voices a term as a fact file constant — a named output file carrying a single atomic value.
The term's anchor names the file; consumers read the value verbatim without parsing.
At detail sites, referenced by
{axhoot_typed_output}
with
{axd_fact}.

[[axpot_tally]]
{axpot_tally}::
Voices a term as a fact map infix — a naming template that produces dynamically-named fact files at runtime.
The term's anchor names the infix constant; the full filename is composed as `«prefix»«INFIX»«suffix»` where prefix and suffix are runtime values.
One file per discovered item; consumers check presence via `test -f` on composed filenames.
At detail sites, referenced by
{axhoot_typed_output}
with
{axd_tally}.

=== New Operation Terms

[[axo_group]]
{axo_group}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named collection of related
{axo_procedure_s}
or
{axo_method_s}
that share an affiliated
{axo_entity}.
The group provides a formal identity at the
{axl_definition_site}
for operation clustering that would otherwise be implicit in naming conventions.

[[axo_method]]
{axo_method}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an executable operation affiliated with a specific
{axo_entity}.
Peer to
{axo_procedure};
both are kinds of operation, neither voices the other.
The entity must appear as the second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the definition text.
This is a grammar constraint: the voicing demands the relationship be stated.

== Axial Hierarchy Operation Markers

{axho*} markers appear at the
{axl_detail_site}
as standalone annotations, paralleling the
{axhr*}
regime hierarchy markers.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text.

=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
|===

[[axhob_operation]]
{axhob_operation}::
Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
{axl_detail_site}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the operation this detail site describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site}.
No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_depot_levy}

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
----

[[axhoc_completion]]
{axhoc_completion}::
Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
Distinct from
{axhog_guarantee}
which describes state promises, and
{axhoot_typed_output}
/{axhoom_motif_output}
which describe produced data.
One per operation.

[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopa_arg_parameter]]
{axhopa_arg_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]
{axhopm_motif_parameter}::
Marks an input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoot_typed_output]]
{axhoot_typed_output}::
Marks a named output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
Parallel to the
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
pattern: no new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the output's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., an output that is simply a list of names or a CIDR block).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoom_motif_output]]
{axhoom_motif_output}::
Marks an output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
+
May carry
{axd_fact}
to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
{axd_tally}
to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.

[[axhoq_precondition]]
{axhoq_precondition}::
Marks a required precondition in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.

[[axhog_guarantee]]
{axhog_guarantee}::
Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.

[[axhos_step]]
{axhos_step}::
Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
{axhos_waymark}
anchor.

[[axhos_waymark]]
{axhos_waymark}::
Variant of
{axhos_step}
for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.

=== Nesting Rules

Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* {axhob_operation}
opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
{axhob_operation}
* {axhoc_completion},
{axhopt_typed_parameter},
{axhopa_arg_parameter},
{axhopm_motif_parameter},
{axhoot_typed_output},
{axhoom_motif_output},
{axhoq_precondition},
{axhog_guarantee},
and
{axhos_step}
must appear within
{axhob_operation}
scope
* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
* At most one
{axhoc_completion}
per operation

=== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhob_operation}
attribute reference must match an operation defined with
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site}
* {axhob_operation}
carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only
* When a
{axvo_method}
uses
{axd_grouped},
the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity

== Axial Voicing Entity Terms

Three annotation families enable entity specification across
{axl_definition_site_s}
and
{axl_detail_site_s},
paralleling the regime and operation annotation patterns.

Entity specifications describe objects with both structure (fields) and behavior (methods).
Methods within an entity may be *scoped* or *elevated*:

* A *scoped* method is defined entirely within the entity subdocument.
It has no parent-document linked term — its identity exists only within the entity's contract, expressed through position and prose.

* An *elevated* method possesses a linked term at the
{axl_definition_site}
in the parent document, voiced with
{axvo_method}.
Elevated methods earn independent addressability when other spec layers (procedures, dispatch protocols) need to reference them.

A well-constrained entity is its own documentation.
Scoped methods are the default; elevation is the exception for methods that must be independently addressable.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axve_*)

{axve_*} annotations appear at the
{axl_definition_site}
between anchor and definition, declaring that the term defines an entity.

[[axve_entity]]
{axve_entity}::
Voices an
{axo_entity}
definition.
The annotated term defines an object with both structure and behavior whose contract is elaborated in a subdocument via
{axhe*}
hierarchy markers.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the entity being defined.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
optional
{axd_internal}.

=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhe*_)

Hierarchy markers appear in entity subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form.
Markers with non-zero arity read ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text.
Zero-arity markers carry their type and dimension information entirely on the annotation line.

The entity hierarchy uses a two-layer structure:

* *Layer 1*: Entity scope opened by
{axheb_entity},
containing field markers
({axheft_typed_field},
{axhefm_motif_field})
and method markers
({axhems_scoped_method},
{axheme_elevated_method}).

* *Layer 2*: Method scope opened by a method marker, containing parameter markers
({axhempt_typed_parameter},
{axhempa_arg_parameter},
{axhempm_motif_parameter})
and output markers
({axhemot_typed_output},
{axhemom_motif_output}).

Variant suffixes identify the source of type identity:

* `_typed_*` — 1-arity; lookahead reads a project-specific type term
* `_arg_*` — 1-arity; lookahead reads a shared argument term
* `_motif_*` — 0-arity; type expressed through axial motif terms on the annotation line

==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axheb_entity}             |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The entity this subdoc describes
|{axheft_typed_field}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A field's type (domain type term)
|{axhefm_motif_field}       |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
|{axhems_scoped_method}     |1 {mcm_inlay}                |Implementation method name; opens Layer 2
|{axheme_elevated_method}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The parent-document method term
|{axhempt_typed_parameter}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A parameter's type (domain type term)
|{axhempa_arg_parameter}    |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A parameter's identity (shared argument term)
|{axhempm_motif_parameter}  |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
|{axhemot_typed_output}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An output's type (domain type term)
|{axhemom_motif_output}     |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
|===

[[axheb_entity]]
{axheb_entity}::
Opens the entity hierarchy block at the
{axl_detail_site}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the entity this detail site describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axve_entity}
at the
{axl_definition_site}.

[[axheft_typed_field]]
{axheft_typed_field}::
Marks a data member of the entity whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor.
{axd_dimension_s}
on the annotation line qualify the field:
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional},
{axd_repeated},
etc.

[[axhefm_motif_field]]
{axhefm_motif_field}::
Marks a data member of the entity whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
0-arity — no lookahead.
{axd_dimension_s}
on the annotation line include a type motif
({axt_string},
{axt_boolean},
{axt_array},
etc.) and structural qualifiers
({axd_required},
{axd_repeated},
etc.).

[[axhems_scoped_method]]
{axhems_scoped_method}::
Opens a scoped method scope within the entity.
The method has no parent-document
{mcm_quoin}
— its identity is an
{mcm_inlay}
that exists only within the entity subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_inlay}
— the implementation method name, recognized by its prefix pattern.
Opens Layer 2 scope for parameter and output markers.

[[axheme_elevated_method]]
{axheme_elevated_method}::
References an elevated method within the entity — a method that has a linked term at the
{axl_definition_site}
in the parent document, annotated with
{axvo_method}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the parent-document method term.
Opens Layer 2 scope for parameter and output markers.

[[axhempt_typed_parameter]]
{axhempt_typed_parameter}::
Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor.
{axd_dimension_s}
on the annotation line qualify the parameter:
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional},
etc.

[[axhempa_arg_parameter]]
{axhempa_arg_parameter}::
Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose identity reuses a shared argument term.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing shared argument anchor.

[[axhempm_motif_parameter]]
{axhempm_motif_parameter}::
Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
0-arity — no lookahead.
{axd_dimension_s}
on the annotation line include a type motif and structural qualifiers.

[[axhemot_typed_output]]
{axhemot_typed_output}::
Marks an output of the current method scope whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor.

[[axhemom_motif_output]]
{axhemom_motif_output}::
Marks an output of the current method scope whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
0-arity — no lookahead.
{axd_dimension_s}
on the annotation line include a type motif and structural qualifiers.

==== Nesting Rules

Entity hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* {axheb_entity}
opens the entity block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end
* {axheft_typed_field}
and
{axhefm_motif_field}
must appear within
{axheb_entity}
scope but outside any method scope (Layer 1)
* {axhems_scoped_method}
and
{axheme_elevated_method}
must appear within
{axheb_entity}
scope (Layer 1); each opens a new method scope (Layer 2)
* A method marker implicitly closes any prior method scope
* {axhempt_typed_parameter},
{axhempa_arg_parameter},
{axhempm_motif_parameter},
{axhemot_typed_output},
and
{axhemom_motif_output}
must appear within a method scope (Layer 2)
* Field markers should precede method markers within the entity (fields then methods)

==== Cross-Document Validation

* {axheb_entity}
attribute reference must match an entity defined with
{axve_entity}
at the
{axl_definition_site}
* {axheme_elevated_method}
attribute reference must match a method defined with
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site},
and that method's affiliated entity must match the entity opened by
{axheb_entity}

== Axial Type Universal Terms

{axtu_*} types represent generic infrastructure patterns that are not specific to any cloud provider or platform.
These types capture critical gestalts that appear across many systems.
Platform-specific nuances (e.g., path separators on Windows vs Unix) are handled through
{axl_voicing_s}.

[[axtu_string]]
{axtu_string}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a generic bounded string value.
{axl_voicing_s}
specify minimum and maximum length constraints appropriate to their context.

[[axtu_path]]
{axtu_path}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a file system path.
Platform differences (path separators, root conventions) are handled by
{axl_voicing_s}.
May be absolute or relative depending on context.

[[axtu_ipv4]]
{axtu_ipv4}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an IPv4 address in dotted-decimal notation.
Format: four octets 0-255 separated by dots (e.g., `192.168.1.1`).

[[axtu_cidr]]
{axtu_cidr}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a CIDR block for network addressing.
Format: IPv4 address followed by slash and prefix length (e.g., `10.0.0.0/8`).

[[axtu_port]]
{axtu_port}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a network port number.
Valid range: 1-65535. Well-known ports are 1-1023.

[[axtu_domain]]
{axtu_domain}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a DNS domain name.
Must start and end with alphanumeric characters; may contain dots and hyphens.

[[axtu_sha256]]
{axtu_sha256}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a SHA-256 cryptographic hash.
Format: 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.

[[axtu_xname]]
{axtu_xname}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a cross-context safe identifier.
Must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.
Safe for use in file names, environment variables, and most programming contexts.

== Axial Type Google Terms

{axtg_*} types represent Google Cloud Platform resource identifiers and patterns.
These types have specific format requirements enforced by GCP APIs.

[[axtg_project_id]]
{axtg_project_id}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud Platform project identifier.
Format: 6-30 lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must start with a letter and end with letter or digit.
Globally unique across all of GCP.

[[axtg_region]]
{axtg_region}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud Platform region code.
Format: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (e.g., `us-central1`, `europe-west1`).
Determines geographic location for regional resources.

[[axtg_service_account]]
{axtg_service_account}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud Platform service account email.
Format: `{account-id}@{project-id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` where account-id is 6-30 characters.
Used as identity for programmatic API access.

[[axtg_billing_account]]
{axtg_billing_account}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud Platform billing account identifier.
Format: `XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX` (three groups of six uppercase alphanumeric characters).
Links projects to payment sources.

[[axtg_project_number]]
{axtg_project_number}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud Platform project number.
Format: numeric identifier automatically assigned at project creation.
Globally unique across all of GCP.
Immutable once assigned.

== Axial Infrastructure Google Terms

{axig_*} terms represent Google Cloud Platform infrastructure patterns.
Each term explicitly declares its level:

* **Service-level**: the managed platform itself
* **Instance-level**: a particular resource within a service
* **Operational**: execution patterns and states

=== Service-Level Terms

[[axig_resource_manager]]
{axig_resource_manager}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing Google Cloud Resource Manager.
Service-level: the platform for managing organizations, folders, and projects.

[[axig_cloud_storage]]
{axig_cloud_storage}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing Google Cloud Storage.
Service-level: the object storage platform, not buckets within it.

[[axig_artifact_registry]]
{axig_artifact_registry}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing Google Artifact Registry.
Service-level: the artifact management platform, not repositories within it.

[[axig_cloud_build]]
{axig_cloud_build}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing Google Cloud Build.
Service-level: the build execution platform, not individual builds.

[[axig_iam]]
{axig_iam}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing Google Cloud IAM.
Service-level: the identity and access management platform.

=== Instance-Level: Resource Manager

[[axig_organization]]
{axig_organization}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud organization.
Instance-level: a particular organization, top of GCP resource hierarchy.

[[axig_folder]]
{axig_folder}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud folder.
Instance-level: an organizational container within an
{axig_organization}.

[[axig_project]]
{axig_project}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud project.
Instance-level: a resource container with billing, APIs, and IAM.

[[axig_lien]]
{axig_lien}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Google Cloud lien.
Instance-level: a deletion protection on a resource.

=== Instance-Level: Cloud Storage

[[axig_bucket]]
{axig_bucket}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Cloud Storage bucket.
Instance-level: an object container with ACLs and lifecycle policies.

=== Instance-Level: Artifact Registry

[[axig_artifact_repository]]
{axig_artifact_repository}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an Artifact Registry repository.
Instance-level: container for container images or generic artifacts.

[[axig_container_image]]
{axig_container_image}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a container image in Artifact Registry.
Instance-level: an image with tags and digest.

[[axig_generic_artifact]]
{axig_generic_artifact}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a generic artifact in Artifact Registry.
Instance-level: a non-container artifact (archive, binary, metadata).

=== Instance-Level: Cloud Build

[[axig_build]]
{axig_build}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Cloud Build execution.
Instance-level: a particular build processing instructions to create artifacts.

[[axig_build_trigger]]
{axig_build_trigger}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Cloud Build trigger.
Instance-level: a configured trigger that dispatches builds in response to events or API calls.

[[axig_build_config]]
{axig_build_config}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a build configuration file.
Instance-level: a committed YAML or JSON file defining build steps, images, and artifacts.

=== Instance-Level: Cloud Build v2

[[axig_cbv2_connection]]
{axig_cbv2_connection}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Cloud Build v2 connection.
Instance-level: an authenticated bridge from GCP to a GitHub organization via the "Google Cloud Build" GitHub App, with credentials stored in Secret Manager.

[[axig_cbv2_repository]]
{axig_cbv2_repository}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Cloud Build v2 repository resource within a connection.
Instance-level: a binding that grants Cloud Build access to a specific external repository.

// RETIRED: axig_repo_link — replaced by axig_cbv2_repository (2026-03-02)

=== Instance-Level: Build Provenance

[[axig_slsa_provenance]]
{axig_slsa_provenance}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing SLSA build provenance.
Instance-level: a cryptographically signed attestation recording build inputs, environment, and outputs per the SLSA framework.

=== Instance-Level: IAM

[[axig_service_account]]
{axig_service_account}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a GCP service account.
Instance-level: a non-human identity for API authentication.

[[axig_role]]
{axig_role}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a GCP IAM role.
Instance-level: a named collection of permissions.

[[axig_binding]]
{axig_binding}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a GCP IAM policy binding.
Instance-level: association of identity to role on a resource scope.

[[axig_service_account_key]]
{axig_service_account_key}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a service account key file.
Instance-level: JSON credential for service account authentication.

=== Operational Terms

[[axig_lro]]
{axig_lro}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Long Running Operation.
Operational: async pattern returning handle for later completion check.

[[axig_project_state]]
{axig_project_state}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a project lifecycle state.
Operational: conditions like ACTIVE, DELETE_REQUESTED describing status.

== Axial Key Premise Terms

[[axk_premise]]
{axk_premise}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a declared design constraint or assumption that explicitly bounds system complexity.
Premises eliminate classes of problems by stating what the system will not handle, simplifying design.

== Infrastructure Terms

[[axo_dependency]]
{axo_dependency}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing required component or library for operation.
Specifies external requirements for functionality.

== Compliance Rules

This section specifies compositional requirements between motifs.
When a term voices a particular motif, these rules define what companion elements are required.

=== Operation Detail-Site Completeness

A term voiced
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
with a detail-site subdocument requires:

* {axhob_operation}
— opens the hierarchy block, with lookahead to the operation's linked term

* At least one
{axhos_step}
— what the operation does

* {axhoc_completion}
— how the operation signals success or failure

Optional markers that MAY be included:

* {axhopt_typed_parameter}
/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
/{axhopm_motif_parameter}
— input parameters (typed/arg variants use 1-arity lookahead; motif is bare)

* {axhoot_typed_output}
/{axhoom_motif_output}
— produced artifacts (typed variant uses 1-arity lookahead; motif is bare)

* {axhoq_precondition}
— environmental state requirements before execution

* {axhog_guarantee}
— environmental state guarantees after success

=== CLI Subcommand Completeness

Voicing as
{axi_cli_subcommand}
requires:

* Exit Status section voiced as
{axa_exit_uniform}
or
{axa_exit_enumerated}
(which voice
{axhoc_completion}
in CLI context)

* Arguments section voiced as
{axa_argument_list}
(which voices
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
in CLI context; may list zero arguments if operation takes none)

=== CLI Option Completeness

Voicing as
{axa_cli_option}
requires the definition text to specify:

* Long form (e.g., `--file`)

* Short form or explicit absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")

* Default value, if the argument is used with
{axd_defaulted}

=== Argument List Item Qualification

Within an
{axa_argument_list},
each item requires a preceding
{axd_dimension}
annotation specifying at minimum:

* {axd_required}
or
{axd_optional}

* {axd_defaulted}
if a default value applies

=== Type Voicing Qualification

Voicing a type (subspecializing
{axt_*}
or
{axtu_*}
motifs) constrains dimension usage.

Valid dimensions:

* {axd_list}
— type intrinsically represents delimited collection

Invalid dimensions:

* {axd_required},
{axd_optional},
{axd_defaulted}
— presence applies to usage sites, not type definitions

* {axd_repeated}
— repetition applies to usage sites, not type definitions

=== Regime Specification Completeness

Voicing as
{axrg_regime}
requires:

* Format dimension (
{axf_bash},
{axf_makefile},
{axf_json},
etc.) specifying how assignments are carried

* Provenance dimension (
{axrd_file_sourced},
{axrd_env_inherited},
or
{axrd_constructed})
declaring how
{axrg_assignment_s}
arrive

=== Regime Variable Qualification

Voicing as
{axrg_variable}
requires:

* Type in annotation (base type motif or type voicing)

* At minimum
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional}

* {axd_defaulted}
if a default value applies

Valid dimensions:

* {axd_required}
— variable must have assignment

* {axd_optional}
— variable may be absent

* {axd_defaulted}
— fallback value when absent

* {axd_list}
— inherited from type voicing; variable's type is a delimited collection

Invalid dimensions:

* {axd_repeated}
— regime files use unique keys; multiple assignments of same variable not permitted

* {axd_nullable}
— format-dependent;
{axf_bash}
has no null distinct from empty string

=== Regime Voicing Annotation Completeness

{axvr_regime}
requires:

* {axf_format}
dimension specifying how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried

{axvr_variable}
requires:

* Second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be the parent regime

* {axd_required}
or
{axd_optional}

* Type
{axl_motif}
(base type or type voicing)

{axvr_group}
requires:

* Second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be the parent regime

=== Regime Hierarchy Consistency

* {axvr_group}
with
{axd_conditional}
requires corresponding
{axhrgb_group}
in subdocument to have at least one
{axhrgc_gate}

* {axvr_group}
without
{axd_conditional}:
corresponding
{axhrgb_group}
must have no
{axhrgc_gate}

* Every
{axvr_variable}
under a regime should appear as either
{axhrv_variable}
or
{axhrgv_variable}
in the subdocument

=== Operation Voicing Annotation Completeness

{axvo_procedure}
requires:

* Exactly one lifecycle dimension:
{axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}

* If
{axd_grouped}:
second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be an
{axo_group}

{axvo_method}
requires:

* Second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be the affiliated
{axo_entity}

* Exactly one lifecycle dimension:
{axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}

* If
{axd_grouped}:
third
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be an
{axo_group}

{axvo_group}
requires:

* Second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in definition text must be the affiliated
{axo_entity}

* Optional exposure dimension:
{axd_tabtarget},
{axd_slash_command},
or other interface mechanism specifying the default exposure for external members

=== Operation Hierarchy Consistency

* {axhob_operation}
attribute reference must match a term voiced
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site}

* Every
{axhoc_completion},
{axhopt_typed_parameter},
{axhopa_arg_parameter},
{axhopm_motif_parameter},
{axhoot_typed_output},
{axhoom_motif_output},
{axhoq_precondition},
{axhog_guarantee},
and
{axhos_step}
must appear within
{axhob_operation}
scope

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// axc_: Axial Control (orchestration control terms) → mcm_form_deflist
// axe_: Axial Environment (execution context patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axo_: Axial Operation (operation patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_section for sequences
// axt_: Axial Type (type system definitions) → mcm_form_deflist
// axr_: Axial Record (record/member structure patterns) → mcm_form_section for records, mcm_form_deflist for members
// axj_: Axial JSON (JSON-specific concerns) → mcm_form_section for structures, mcm_form_bullet for fields
// axa_: Axial Argument (command argument patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_bullet under parent
// axi_: Axial Interface (interface patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axk_: Axial Key Premise (design constraints) → mcm_form_deflist
// axl_: Axial Lexicon (motif vocabulary terms) → mcm_form_deflist
// axs_: Axial Section (documentation section motifs) → mcm_form_deflist
// axd_: Axial Dimension (member/argument characteristic modifiers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axf_: Axial Format (serialization/carriage formats) → mcm_form_deflist
// axrg_: Axial Regime (configuration system patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axvr_: Axial Voicing Regime (definition-site regime annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axvo_: Axial Voicing Operation (definition-site operation annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axhr*_: Axial Hierarchy Regime (standalone hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axho*_: Axial Hierarchy Operation (standalone operation hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axpo*_: Axial Product-of-Operation (definition-site voicings for operation outputs) → mcm_form_deflist
// axve_: Axial Voicing Entity (definition-site entity annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axhe*_: Axial Hierarchy Entity (standalone entity hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// xref_: External specification references
//
// Extended Type Categories (domain-specific type subcategories):
// axtu_: Axial Type Universal (generic infrastructure types) → mcm_form_deflist
// axtg_: Axial Type Google (Google Cloud Platform types) → mcm_form_deflist
// axtw_: Axial Type AWS (Amazon Web Services types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axta_: Axial Type Azure (Microsoft Azure types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axth_: Axial Type GitHub (GitHub types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// Reserved: axtc_ (Cloudflare), axti_ (IBM), axtm_ (avoid - Microsoft confusion)
//
// Extended Infrastructure Categories (domain-specific infrastructure patterns):
// axig_: Axial Infrastructure Google (GCP infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist
// axiw_: Axial Infrastructure AWS (AWS infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axia_: Axial Infrastructure Azure (Azure infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axih_: Axial Infrastructure GitHub (GitHub infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
